D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Menu Pricing Colour Cues — Red Highlights and Anchors

Restaurant studies: dropping currency symbol (24 vs $24) and using a coloured anchor item raises average ticket size 8–13%; "decoy" red item makes neighbours look like deals

Cornell Hotel Studies (Yang 2009): menus without "$" outsell ones with by ~8%. Highlight colour creates an anchor — a red-priced $42 entrée frames adjacent $24 entrées as bargains. Casino menus and Lunchclub use this aggressively.

Toggle decoy anchor on/off + slide its price.
